general_skills.title: Verify Before Mutating
general_skills.principle: Always read the current state of an Odoo record before creating, updating, or deleting it. Confirm the operation with the user when it affects production data.
general_skills.application: Before any write/create/delete call, do a search_read to confirm the target exists and is in the expected state. Ask for explicit user confirmation on destructive operations.

general_skills.title: Use Domain Filters Precisely
general_skills.principle: Construct Odoo domains with the most specific filters possible to avoid retrieving excessive data or acting on wrong records.
general_skills.application: Always include state, type, and date filters when querying models like account.move, stock.picking, or sale.order. Prefer exact matches over broad searches.

general_skills.title: Check Model Availability
general_skills.principle: Not all Odoo models are available on every instance — modules may not be installed. Verify model existence before querying.
general_skills.application: Use odoo_list_models or odoo_get_fields to confirm the model exists. If a model is missing, explain which module needs to be installed.

general_skills.title: Batch Operations Safely
general_skills.principle: When processing multiple records, work in small batches and report progress. Never process unbounded result sets in a single call.
general_skills.application: Limit search_read results with a reasonable limit (50-100). For write operations on many records, process in chunks of 10-20 and confirm intermediate results.

general_skills.title: Interpret Odoo Errors Contextually
general_skills.principle: Odoo errors often contain technical tracebacks. Translate them into actionable explanations for the user, identifying the root cause and suggesting fixes.
general_skills.application: When an RPC call fails, parse the error message for common patterns (access rights, missing fields, constraint violations) and explain in plain language.

general_skills.title: Respect the Odoo Workflow
general_skills.principle: Odoo records follow state machines (draft -> confirmed -> done). Never skip states by directly writing state fields — use the appropriate action methods.
general_skills.application: Use action_post for invoices, action_confirm for sale orders, button_validate for stock pickings. Never write {'state': 'posted'} directly.

general_skills.title: Progressive Field Discovery
general_skills.principle: When working with unfamiliar models or investigating data quality, use odoo_get_fields first to understand available fields before querying records.
general_skills.application: Start with odoo_get_fields to see field types and relationships, then construct targeted search_read queries with only the fields needed for analysis.

general_skills.title: Multi-Angle Investigation
general_skills.principle: For thorough analysis, examine the same issue from multiple perspectives using related models and cross-references.
general_skills.application: When checking module states, also query ir.module.module.dependency for dependency issues. When auditing cron jobs, check both ir.cron and ir.logging for execution history.

general_skills.title: Combine Diagnostic Tools Strategically
general_skills.principle: Use odoo_doctor as the initial broad sweep, then follow up with targeted model queries to drill into specific findings. This two-phase approach is more efficient than single-tool queries.
general_skills.application: Start with odoo_doctor to get a system overview. Based on its output, use odoo_search_read on specific models (ir.cron, ir.module.module, ir.logging) to investigate flagged areas in detail.

general_skills.title: Aggregate and Summarize for Clarity
general_skills.principle: When presenting findings from multiple queries, organize results into categorized tables or summaries (OK / Warning / Critical) to help users quickly identify priorities.
general_skills.application: Group related findings by severity level. Present counts, totals, and key metrics in tabular format. Highlight critical items first, then warnings, then informational items.

general_skills.title: Temporal Filtering for Audit Trails
general_skills.principle: When investigating recent events or changes, use date/time filters to narrow scope and improve query performance. Always specify time windows for audit queries.
general_skills.application: Use create_date, write_date, or custom date fields with comparison operators (>, <, >=, <=). For 24-hour lookbacks, calculate datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=24). Include timezone awareness in date comparisons.

general_skills.title: Leverage search_count for Efficiency
general_skills.principle: When you only need to know the quantity of records matching a condition, use search_count instead of search_read to reduce data transfer and improve performance.
general_skills.application: Use odoo_search_count to get record counts before deciding whether to fetch full details. Useful for reporting metrics, detecting anomalies (e.g., 'are there any negative stock records?'), and validating data thresholds.

general_skills.title: Combine Count and Detail Queries Strategically
general_skills.principle: Use search_count first to assess scope, then conditionally fetch details only if counts warrant investigation. This reduces unnecessary data transfer.
general_skills.application: Query search_count with a filter to get magnitude of issue. If count is high, fetch details with limit and offset. If count is zero, report 'no issues found' immediately without additional queries.

general_skills.title: Cross-Reference Related Models for Completeness
general_skills.principle: When investigating a specific model (e.g., ir.cron), always check related audit/logging models (ir.logging) to get the full picture of recent activity and failures.
general_skills.application: For cron jobs, query both ir.cron (state) and ir.logging (execution history). For modules, query both ir.module.module (state) and ir.module.module.dependency (dependency health). This reveals hidden issues.

general_skills.title: Build Dependency Graphs Iteratively
general_skills.principle: When exploring dependency relationships (e.g., module dependencies), query parent-child relationships iteratively rather than trying to fetch the entire tree at once.
general_skills.application: Start with a target module, fetch its direct dependencies via ir.module.module.dependency. For each dependency, recursively fetch its dependencies. Use a visited set to avoid cycles. Present results as a hierarchical structure or adjacency list.

general_skills.title: Validate Data Consistency Across Related Models
general_skills.principle: When investigating module or system state, cross-validate findings across multiple related models to detect inconsistencies that indicate deeper issues.
general_skills.application: For module checks: verify state in ir.module.module matches expected installation status, check ir.module.module.dependency for broken links, and query ir.model for orphaned model definitions. Report any mismatches as warnings.

general_skills.title: Optimize Query Sequences with Early Termination
general_skills.principle: Structure diagnostic queries to fail fast: use search_count to detect zero-result conditions before fetching full records, avoiding unnecessary data transfer.
general_skills.application: When investigating a category of issues (e.g., error logs), first search_count to determine if any records exist. If count is 0, report immediately. If count is high, fetch with limit. This pattern reduces wasted queries.

general_skills.title: Comprehensive Related Data Auditing
general_skills.principle: When asked to be thorough on a data category, systematically query all related and dependent models to provide complete picture of data health.
general_skills.application: For sales data: query sale.order, sale.order.line, account.move (invoices), stock.picking, and account.payment. For partners: query res.partner, res.partner.address, account.move (as customer/vendor). Use search_count on each to report totals and anomalies.

general_skills.title: Leverage Specialized Configuration Tools
general_skills.principle: Use domain-specific tools (odoo_set_default, odoo_modify_action) for configuration tasks rather than generic write operations. These tools handle Odoo-specific logic correctly.
general_skills.application: For setting field defaults, use odoo_set_default with model and field names. For modifying window actions (sorting, filtering), use odoo_modify_action. These tools abstract away ir.default and ir.actions.act_window complexity.

general_skills.title: Validate Configuration Changes with Model Info
general_skills.principle: After setting defaults or modifying actions, use odoo_model_info or targeted search_read to verify the configuration was applied correctly.
general_skills.application: After odoo_set_default, query ir.default to confirm the record was created. After odoo_modify_action, query ir.actions.act_window to verify the context or domain was updated. This prevents silent failures.

task_specific_skills.title: Pre-Backup System Inventory
task_specific_skills.principle: Before backup operations, gather comprehensive system metrics to document baseline state and enable post-restore validation.
task_specific_skills.application: Use odoo_doctor for system overview, then systematically count records in core models (res.partner, sale.order, account.move, stock.picking, ir.attachment). Document database name, Odoo version, and key metrics. This creates a restore checklist.

task_specific_skills.title: Filestore Size Estimation
task_specific_skills.principle: Estimate backup size by analyzing ir.attachment records, which correlate with filestore disk usage.
task_specific_skills.application: Use search_count on ir.attachment with date filters to estimate total attachments. Query ir.attachment with fields=['file_size'] to calculate total bytes. This helps plan backup storage and restore time.

task_specific_skills.title: Pending Operations Detection
task_specific_skills.principle: Before restore, identify any in-progress module installations, upgrades, or scheduled jobs that could interfere with the restore process.
task_specific_skills.application: Query ir.module.module for state='to install' or 'to upgrade'. Query ir.cron for active=True and next_call in near future. Query ir.logging for recent errors. These indicate operations that should complete or be disabled before restore.

task_specific_skills.title: Scheduled Action Audit for Restore Safety
task_specific_skills.principle: List all active scheduled actions (cron jobs) to determine which should be disabled during restore to prevent data conflicts.
task_specific_skills.application: Query ir.cron with active=True, fetch fields=['name', 'model_name', 'state', 'nextcall', 'numbercall']. Identify critical jobs (payment processing, invoice generation) that must be paused. Document for post-restore re-enablement.

task_specific_skills.title: Global Default Value Management via ir.default
task_specific_skills.principle: Use ir.default model to set field-level defaults that apply across all users and contexts, avoiding hardcoding defaults in code.
task_specific_skills.application: Use odoo_set_default(model, field, value) to create ir.default records. Query ir.default with search_read to audit existing defaults. Defaults apply when creating new records unless overridden by user input or context.

task_specific_skills.title: Window Action Customization for UX
task_specific_skills.principle: Modify ir.actions.act_window to change list view sorting, filtering, and context without altering the underlying model or view definition.
task_specific_skills.application: Use odoo_modify_action to update context (default filters), domain (record filtering), or view_ids (view order). Common use cases: set default sort order, pre-filter by user/company, hide inactive records. Changes apply globally to all users accessing that action.

task_specific_skills.title: Configuration Verification Post-Modification
task_specific_skills.principle: After applying configuration changes (defaults, actions), verify the changes persisted and are correctly formatted in the database.
task_specific_skills.application: After odoo_set_default, query ir.default to confirm the record exists with correct model, field, and value. After odoo_modify_action, query ir.actions.act_window to verify context/domain JSON is valid. Use odoo_model_info to cross-check field definitions.

common_mistakes.mistake: Skipping field discovery before querying unfamiliar models
common_mistakes.consequence: Queries fail or return incomplete data because field names are guessed incorrectly or required fields are missing.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always call odoo_get_fields first on unfamiliar models to understand structure, field types, and relationships before constructing search_read queries.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using search_read when search_count would suffice
common_mistakes.consequence: Unnecessary data transfer and slower response times when only record counts are needed for reporting or anomaly detection.
common_mistakes.prevention: For questions like 'how many records exist?', 'are there any X?', or 'what's the total?', use search_count first. Only fetch full records if counts indicate further investigation is needed.

common_mistakes.consequence: Incomplete analysis that misses root causes or related issues. For example, checking ir.cron state without checking ir.logging for execution failures.
common_mistakes.prevention: When investigating a model, always identify and query related audit/logging models. Use GS-008 (Multi-Angle Investigation) and GS-014 (Cross-Reference Related Models) patterns.

common_mistakes.mistake: Writing state fields directly instead of using workflow action methods
common_mistakes.consequence: Workflow constraints violated, related fields not updated, and data integrity compromised. For example, writing {'state': 'posted'} on account.move instead of calling action_post.
common_mistakes.prevention: Always use the appropriate action method (action_post, action_confirm, button_validate) to transition record states. Never write state fields directly.

common_mistakes.mistake: Fetching entire record sets without limits or filters
common_mistakes.consequence: Timeout errors, memory exhaustion, and poor user experience when result sets are large (thousands of records).
common_mistakes.prevention: Always include limit parameter in search_read (50-100 records). Use precise domain filters (state, date ranges, type). For large operations, use GS-004 (Batch Operations Safely) pattern.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not confirming user intent before destructive operations
common_mistakes.consequence: Accidental data loss or unintended modifications that affect production data and user workflows.
common_mistakes.prevention: Before any write/create/delete operation, verify current state with search_read and ask explicit user confirmation. Use GS-001 (Verify Before Mutating) pattern.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using generic write operations for configuration instead of specialized tools
common_mistakes.consequence: Configuration changes fail silently or are applied incorrectly because Odoo-specific logic is bypassed. For example, writing ir.default directly instead of using odoo_set_default.
common_mistakes.prevention: Use specialized tools: odoo_set_default for field defaults, odoo_modify_action for window action changes. These tools handle Odoo's internal logic correctly.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not validating configuration changes after applying them
common_mistakes.consequence: Configuration appears to succeed but is not actually persisted or is malformed, leading to unexpected behavior.
common_mistakes.prevention: After odoo_set_default or odoo_modify_action, query the affected model (ir.default, ir.actions.act_window) to confirm the change was applied correctly. Use GS-020 pattern.

common_mistakes.mistake: Assuming all Odoo modules are installed on every instance
common_mistakes.consequence: Queries fail with 'model not found' errors when querying models from optional modules (e.g., account.move on non-accounting instances).
common_mistakes.prevention: Use odoo_list_models or odoo_get_fields to verify model existence before querying. If a model is missing, identify and explain which module needs to be installed.

common_mistakes.mistake: Performing single large query instead of strategic count-then-detail approach
common_mistakes.consequence: Wasted queries and data transfer when investigating whether an issue exists. For example, fetching all error logs when only needing to know if errors exist.
common_mistakes.prevention: Use GS-013 (Combine Count and Detail Queries Strategically) pattern: search_count first to assess scope, then conditionally fetch details only if warranted.
